nodes.label: Interface: Live AR + 2D Correction (the centerpiece)
nodes.rationale: DRAFT god-tier interactive experience, supersedes the flat sheet-left/camera-right layout in docs/16; keeps docs/16's law: every pixel is a sense the Deaf learner does not have (cool == useful). Two coupled views: live-AR Beat-Saber play on the real neck + a dead-simple 2D fretboard showing what each finger did wrong.

nodes.label: Live AR play — Beat-Saber depth-approach on the real fretboard
nodes.rationale: Notes fly in from depth (Z axis) and land on the exact (string, fret) cell on the beat, arriving where the finger goes (killing the chart-to-hand translation). Registration via ArUco on the headstock (+ optional body marker) to per-frame 6-DoF neck pose, temporally smoothed (one-euro/Kalman), rendered in the neck coordinate frame. Color = per-string (6 colors) same palette as the back body-zones. Sustain = a depth tail; chord = notes together (bloom) or strum sweep.

nodes.label: Virtual-neck fallback (demo insurance — build FIRST)
nodes.rationale: A virtual-neck mirror: the identical Z-axis renderer with a swappable backdrop (live camera+ArUco when pose is solid, a clean rendered neck when it isn't). Same experience, only backdrop + pose source change; jittery AR looks worse than none, so the centerpiece can never die on stage.

nodes.label: Targeted 2D correction fretboard (what you played vs target ghost, animated fix)
nodes.rationale: On a miss, rewind the segment and switch to a top-down 2D fretboard schematic: per finger show what you played (string, fret, sub-fret distance d, pressure dot hollow/solid/over-saturated) + the target as ghost dots + dead-simple animated arrows / press-down pulses in ASL-gloss-friendly language. The correction view IS the docs/20 fusion output, drawn.

nodes.label: Per-finger placement (solid, vision) + pressure (vision-led, audio cross-checked)
nodes.rationale: Per-finger placement (which finger, where, wire-distance d) is solid from vision; per-finger pressure is a vision-led estimate (finger-load geometry) cross-checked by the global audio buzz - robust for single notes, best-effort attribution in chords; show a hint not a fake precise number.

nodes.label: One phase-locked signal coherence (screen + body + sound from one stream)
nodes.rationale: The AR notes, the body-aurora ambient, and the haptic drive all come from one stream; nail a note and all channels confirm together - the alive, not an app feeling.

edges.target: ArUco marker (4x4_50, id 0, ~30 mm, on headstock)
